channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Inside how podcasters moved the voting needle with young men ahead of Trump's massive win
item.description: President-elect Trump joined his first podcast in 2022 with the Nelk Boys, ahead of joining dozens of podcasts and earning support through an initiative called Send the Vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massachusetts lawmakers push for an effort to ban all tobacco sales over time
item.description: A handful of Massachusetts lawmakers are hoping to persuade their colleagues to support a proposal that would make the state the first to adopt a ban meant to eliminate the use of tobacco products over time.
item.dc:creator: Steve Leblanc, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Donald Trump's call for 'energy dominance' is likely to run into real-world limits
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is creating a National Energy Council that he says will establish U.S. “energy dominance” around the world.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic lawmakers from Connecticut report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut have been targeted by bomb threats on their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The outlook is uncertain for AI regulations as the US government pivots to full Republican control
item.description: The federal government is about to transition from one that prioritized safeguards for artificial intelligence to one more focused on eliminating red tape.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 7 most competitive Senate races in 2026
item.description: The race for key Senate contests in 2026 is already getting underway, with a few major potential candidates hinting they may join the race.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, bringing together the Facebook founder and the former president who was once banned from that social network.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Republican Miller-Meeks wins reelection after recount in close Iowa congressional race
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Republican Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks won her reelection bid Wednesday after a recount confirmed her lead, helping her party pad its thin majority in the U.S. House and retain control of all four of Iowa’s congressional seats.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump transition says Cabinet picks, appointees were targeted by bomb threats, swatting attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A number of President-elect Donald Trump 's most prominent Cabinet picks and appointees have been targeted by bomb threats and “swatting attacks," Trump's transition team said Wednesday. The FBI said it was investigating.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US sanctions more allies of Maduro over alleged post-election crackdown in Venezuela
item.description: The United States has imposed sanctions on an additional 21 allies of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, accusing them of supporting and carrying out acts of repression following July's disputed presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: End of Trump prosecutions renews scrutiny of DOJ's pace
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith's move to dismiss Donald Trump's two federal indictments has sparked finger-pointing from those eager to see the president-elect held to account, with critics airing their frustrations at the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the courts for the anticlimactic end to the case.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Kevin Hassett as National Economic Council head
item.description: President-elect Trump has chosen former White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett to lead the National Economic Council in his next administration, he said Tuesday. “I am very pleased to announce that Dr. Kevin A.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names speechwriter Vince Haley as Domestic Policy Council chief
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Tuesday night that Vince Haley, his campaign speechwriter, will be the next chief of the Domestic Policy Council (DPC). “As Director of the Domestic Policy Council, Vince will help make life better for ALL Americans, and unify our Country through SUCCESS.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-backed Florida official resigns, announces run for Gaetz seat
item.description: Florida’s Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis resigned from his position and announced his candidacy for former Rep. Matt Gaetz's (R-Fla.) seat in the 1st Congressional District on Monday. In a letter addressed to Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, GARANCE BURKE and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools, crime getting attention in early bills submitted for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are getting a jump on what promises to be a heavy workload for the 83rd Legislative Session in Carson City. So far, 160 bills have been filed.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'border czar' willing to 'jail' Denver mayor over deportation position
item.description: "But, look, me and the Denver mayor, we agree on one thing. He’s willing to go to jail, I’m willing to put him in jail.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Republicans to challenge election result in Shakopee House race in court
item.description: After a recount upheld a DFL candidate’s narrow win, the outcome moves next to the courts for a race that would determine whether the Minnesota House is in GOP hands or tied next year.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Walmart becomes latest — and biggest — company to roll back its DEI policies
item.description: Conservative groups have filed lawsuits against workplace diversity initiatives that prioritize historically marginalized groups.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump aide sought payment to 'promote' potential picks: Reports
item.description: Trump's attorneys are investigating allegations that one of his top aides offered promotion in exchange for money, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Rand Paul: Denver mayor could be removed over deportation resistance
item.description: Denver Mayor Mike Johnston (D) has vowed to protect migrants in his community as the Trump transition team promises it will crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens Canada, Mexico, China with tariffs
item.description: He said the countries weren't doing enough to control the flow of illegal drugs, especially fentanyl, and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Rays must decide fate of stadium deal by Dec. 1, Pinellas commission chair says
item.description: The Pinellas County Commission is seeking answers from the Tampa Bay Rays organization on its embattled stadium deal.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New law forces landlords to notify tenants of bed bug infestations in NY
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new law in New York requires landlords to notify tenants of bed bugs within three days of an infestation. However, landlords are only required to inform renters near the infestation and those directly at risk.
item.dc:creator: Julie Millet, PIX 11

item.title: New York judge calls off Trump hush money sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s sentencing in his New York criminal case will not go forward as planned next week as his attorneys push to dismiss the prosecution following his election victory. Judge Juan Merchan, who oversees the trial proceedings, announced the adjournment in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, PIX 11
